etymology.description: 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + 喿(喿) (zào, "to chirp") = mouth making sounds -> noise
etymology.interpretation: The character 噪 combines the semantic component 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") with the phonetic 喿(喿) (zào, "to chirp"), suggesting vocal noises or sounds made by the mouth, such as chirping or making noise.

examples.chinese: 工地上的噪音很大(工地上的噪音很大)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngdì shàng de zàoyīn hěn dà.
examples.english: The noise at the construction site is very loud.

examples.chinese: 她用耳机遮住了外面的噪音(她用耳機遮住了外面的噪音)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng ěrjī zhē zhùle wàimiàn de zàoyīn.
examples.english: She used headphones to block out the outside noise.

examples.chinese: 公园里有很多鸟的噪声(公園裡有很多鳥的噪聲)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu hěn duō niǎo de zàoshēng.
examples.english: There is a lot of bird noise in the park.
